text: Some Greek manuscripts have ‘we enjoy’ for ‘let us enjoy’.
‘All alike were guilty’; some would translate ‘In him (Adam) all had sinned’.

The sense seems to be, that those who lived between Adam’s time and that of Moses, whatever their sins were, incurred no guilt of disobedience, there being no (revealed) law to disobey. Death is the penalty of disobedience; and the fact that death came to Adam’s immediate descendants must therefore be attributed to Adam’s disobedience, not to their own. St Paul must not be understood as meaning that men are not responsible for their actions where they have no revealed law to guide them; cf. 2.15 above.
